name: cs0x7f/cstimer
zh: csTimer 计时器(integrated)+ 非 WCA 打乱引擎(/scramble/gen 30+ puzzle 走 vendor 进 tools/cstimer-scramble/);部分 /scramble/solver 求解器也直接 wrap cstimer 自带 solver 当引擎(dino = redi IDA*、大金字塔 mpyrso = 两阶段)
en: csTimer & WCA random-state scramble engine; non-WCA puzzle scramble engine for /scramble/gen (vendored in tools/cstimer-scramble/); a few /scramble/solver solvers also wrap cstimer's own solver as the solve engine (Dino = redi IDA*, Master Pyraminx = two-phase)
long_en: professional speedcubing timer & WCA random-state scramble engine; also vendored at tools/cstimer-scramble/ to power non-WCA puzzle generation (gear/ivy/dino/sq2/giga/pyracrystal/heli/curvycopter/cuboids/15-puzzle/bicube/siamese cubes/...) via classic Web Worker bridge; for a few puzzles whose state space is too large for a full BFS/table, cstimer's own solver is wrapped directly as the SOLVING engine in /scramble/solver (Dino = redi IDA*, Master Pyraminx = two-phase); cstimer's megascramble model + poly3dlib geometry (makePuzzle) are also used as the bit-exact piece-model oracle when building from-scratch reductions (e.g. the Helicopter Cube and the Siamese 2×2×2 solvers)

name: cs0x7f/cube555
zh: 5x5 随机状态求解器(server-side daemon 驱动 /scramble/gen 5x5)
en: 5x5 random-state solver (server-side daemon powers /scramble/gen 5x5)
long_en: five-phase reduction 5x5x5 solver — runs as a Java child process under our Hono server

name: cubing/cubing.js
zh: /sim Pyraminx / Skewb / Megaminx 渲染走 cubing.js TwistyPlayer(复刻 alpha.twizzle.net/edit);Alg parser + 各项目 random-state scramble 也复用此库
en: Pyraminx / Skewb / Megaminx in /sim use cubing.js TwistyPlayer (mirrors alpha.twizzle.net/edit); Alg parser + per-event random-state scramble also share this library
long_en: Lucas Garron's cubing.js — Pyraminx / Skewb / Megaminx canvases in `/sim` embed `TwistyPlayer` to mirror the alpha.twizzle.net/edit demo; the `Alg` parser, `randomScrambleForEvent`, and several `PuzzleID` utilities are reused across alg/recon/training pages

long_en: nemesis-relation algorithm reference (client-side TS reimplementation; UI inspired by [nemesizer.com](https://nemesizer.com))

long_en: non-cube WCA puzzle SVG renderer (`sr-puzzlegen` v1.0.4, vendored as `@cuberoot/vendor-sr-puzzlegen`; Square-1, Megaminx, Pyraminx, Skewb — iso / net / top variants) used by `/visualcube`

name: cube.rider.biz/visualcube.php
zh: PHP VisualCube(Cride5 / Yan / Kira 一脉);URL query API + 扩展 mask 形状 + fcs_format_alg 记号 → @cuberoot/visualcube
en: PHP VisualCube (Cride5 / Yan / Kira lineage); URL query API + extended masks + fcs_format_alg notation
long_en: PHP VisualCube (Cride5 / Yan / Kira lineage); source of the URL query API (`pzl`/`alg`/`arw`/`ac`/`view=trans`/`stage` etc), extended mask shapes, and PHP `fcs_format_alg` notation extensions ported into our `@cuberoot/visualcube`

name: speedcubedb.com
zh: 公式库(/algdb 数据源,2x2/3x3/4x4/5x5 alg sets,scraped)
en: Algorithm database powering /algdb
long_en: algorithm database powering `/algdb` (2x2/3x3/4x4/5x5 alg sets — F2L, OLL, PLL, COLL, ZBLL, 1LLL, OLLCP, VLS, parity, L2E, L2C, …; scraped via `scramble-stats-build/bin/scrape_speedcubedb.ts`)

name: speedcubedb.com/analyze
zh: 打乱分析器算法来源(/scramble/analyzer 的 CFOP 解法枚举)
en: Scramble analyzer algorithm (CFOP solution enumerator for /scramble/analyzer)
long_en: CFOP solution-enumeration algorithm (cross + F2L + OLL + PLL) ported into the `/scramble/analyzer` page

name: Jobarion/cubelib + joba.me/mallard
zh: FMC 分步还原链行为参考(/scramble/analyzer 底部 EO→DR→HTR→FR→Finish + NISS;UI 仿 joba.me/mallard,FR 坐标 / 链编排 / NISS 全用 Rust + TS 按其行为自有重写,未拷源码)
en: FMC step-by-step solver reference (EO→DR→HTR→FR→Finish + NISS chain at the bottom of /scramble/analyzer; UI mirrors joba.me/mallard, engine reimplemented from scratch in Rust + TS — no code copied)
long_en: Jobarion's cubelib Rust FMC solver + its joba.me/mallard web UI — behavior & UI reference for the step-by-step EO→DR→HTR→FR→Finish + NISS chain at the bottom of `/scramble/analyzer`. Our FR coordinate, chain orchestrator and NISS logic were reimplemented from scratch in Rust + TypeScript on our own solver engine; no cubelib source was copied

name: trangium/MovecountCoefficient
zh: MCC 公式测速算法(algSpeed.js 已 port 为 TS,/scramble/mcc;MIT)
en: Movecount Coefficient alg-speed metric (algSpeed.js ported to TS for /scramble/mcc; MIT)
long_en: trangium's Movecount Coefficient calculator — the algSpeed.js fingertrick-simulation metric is ported to TypeScript (golden-locked against upstream) to power the `/scramble/mcc` page; UI rebuilt in React

name: trangium/BatchSolver
zh: 批量公式集求解引擎(worker.js 已 port 为 TS,/scramble/batch-solver 与 /scramble/sub-solver;MIT)
en: Batch Solver algset-generation engine (worker.js ported to TS for /scramble/batch-solver and /scramble/sub-solver; MIT)
long_en: trangium's Batch Solver — the worker.js permutation engine (custom puzzle definitions, equivalence reduction, weighted prune/search, adjust handling, scramble DSL) is ported to TypeScript (golden-locked against upstream message-for-message) to power `/scramble/batch-solver`; `/scramble/sub-solver` (Subsolver's successor) runs on the same engine; UIs rebuilt in React

long_en: commutator decomposition / expansion engine (Zixing Wang) re-wrapped as ES module for the `/alg/commutator` tool; algorithm code unchanged from upstream, UI rebuilt in React

name: rullerzhou-afk/clawd-on-desk
zh: 桌面 AI 桌宠;右下角 Clawd 像素螃蟹的动画与交互移植自此(Clawd 形象版权归 Anthropic,非官方粉丝作品)
en: Desktop AI pet; the corner Clawd pixel crab ports its animations + interactions (Clawd character © Anthropic; unofficial fan use)
long_en: desktop pixel pet that reacts to AI coding agents; its Clawd crab artwork + the idle eye-tracking / state-machine interaction model are ported into the site-wide web desk pet. The Clawd character is the property of Anthropic — this is an unofficial fan use.

name: spooncuber/bldtrainer
zh: 三盲训练器引擎 + 各模块移植(/alg/3bld;作者 Zhi Qiao,GPL-3.0)
en: 3BLD trainer engine + modules ported (/alg/3bld; author Zhi Qiao, GPL-3.0)
long_en: 3BLD trainer engine + modules ported — read-code engine, helper / memo / commutator / parity / flip / twist / float trainers and the resources list under `/alg/3bld` (author Zhi Qiao, GPL-3.0)

name: zhucidong/三盲助手
zh: 盲拧助手 App;记忆回想训练器 + 换位子公式库 + 联想词表合并自此
en: Blindfolded helper app; memo recall trainer + commutator library + association words merged
long_en: blindfolded helper Android app; its memo recall trainer, commutator library, and association-word dictionary are merged into the `/alg/3bld` modules

name: AnnikaStein/SkewbPage
zh: Skewb 技巧训练器(SkewbSkills;三模式首层 / L2L 公式 / One-Look 全 port 为 React /alg/skewb-trainer;作者 Annika Stein,Apache-2.0)
en: Skewb Skills trainer (SkewbSkills; three-mode First Layer / L2L Alg / One-Look ported to React at /alg/skewb-trainer; author Annika Stein, Apache-2.0)
long_en: SkewbSkills web trainer — First Layer Trainer, L2L Algorithm Trainer, and One-Looking Trainer (alg database, RubiksSkewb→WCA notation conversion, scramble generation) ported to React for `/alg/skewb-trainer` (author Annika Stein, Apache-2.0)

name: onionhoney/roux-trainers
zh: 桥式训练器(/alg/roux;引擎 + three.js 渲染器全量 port 为 React、去 MUI;作者 Onionhoney,GPL-3.0)
en: Roux trainer (/alg/roux page; engine + three.js renderer fully ported to React, de-MUI'd; author Onionhoney, GPL-3.0)
long_en: Roux-method trainer collection — first-block / analyzer / FS / SS / CMLL / LSE trainers + in-browser Roux solver + three.js cube renderer, ported to React (MUI removed) and embedded under the `/alg/roux` page (author Onionhoney, GPL-3.0)

name: WCA Regulations & 5b5f Visual Guide
zh: /regulation 规则页:检查 / 打乱 / 判定 / 罚时等核心条款整理自 WCA 官方《竞赛规则与指南》;末节「拼装缺陷判定」的 11 张配图 + 文字移植自 WCA Regulations Committee《Regulation 5b5f Visual Guide》(均已注明来源)
en: /regulation page: core articles (inspection / scrambling / judging / penalties) compiled from the official WCA Regulations & Guidelines; the assembly-defect section reproduces the 11 photos + text from the WCA Regulations Committee's Regulation 5b5f Visual Guide (all credited)
long_en: the `/regulation` page summarizes the most-asked WCA Regulations (inspection, scrambling, solved-state misalignment, +2/DNF, time limits, special events, puzzle rules) with diagrams; its final section reproduces, with attribution, the text and all example photos of the WCA Regulations Committee's official 5b5f Visual Guide slide deck on judging partially-detached / not-fully-placed pieces

name: Herbert Kociemba — Two-Phase Algorithm & Cube Explorer
zh: Kociemba 两阶段算法 + 立方坐标模型(corner/edge 排列 / twist / flip 编码、面转表):/scramble/solver 的 3×3 两阶段解(含 crz3a 疯狂 3×3)、STM 最优解器的状态编码、cube48opt 服务端最优解器都基于此;Cube Explorer 是已知唯一的 STM 最优解器(参照)
en: Kociemba's two-phase algorithm + cube coordinate model (corner/edge perm, twist, flip encoders; face-move tables) underpin the /scramble/solver 3×3 two-phase solve (incl. the crz3a Crazy-3×3 solver), the STM optimal solver's state encoding, and the server-side cube48opt optimal solver; Cube Explorer is the only known prior STM-optimal solver (reference)
long_en: Herbert Kociemba's two-phase algorithm and the Singmaster cubie coordinate model (corner/edge permutation ranks, corner-twist / edge-flip ranks, face-move arrays — also as ported in cs0x7f's min2phase.js) are the basis of the site's client-side 3×3 two-phase solver (the derive-scramble flow + the crz3a solver), the new pure-TS 3×3×3 STM optimal solver's state encoding (cross-validated against these tables), and the server-side cube48opt optimal-solve daemon. Cube Explorer (kociemba.org) is the only known pre-existing STM-optimal solver — the reference our STM goal-test (centers must end home) follows

name: Richard E. Korf — IDA* + Pattern Databases
zh: Korf 的 IDA* + 模式数据库(AAAI 1997)+ 滑块 walking-distance 启发:STM 最优解器的角表(8!×3⁷)+ 双 6 棱表(12P6×2⁶)、h=max(...) 可采纳启发,数字华容道 15p 的 walking-distance,cube48opt 3×3 最优解都出自此法
en: Korf's IDA* with pattern databases (AAAI 1997) + the sliding-tile walking-distance heuristic: the STM optimal solver's corner PDB (8!×3⁷) + two 6-edge PDBs (12P6×2⁶) with h = max(...), the 15-puzzle (15p) walking-distance optimal solve, and cube48opt's 3×3 optimal solve all use this method
long_en: Richard E. Korf's 'Finding Optimal Solutions to Rubik's Cube Using Pattern Databases' (AAAI 1997) — max/additive pattern-database IDA* — is the method behind the pure-TS 3×3×3 STM optimal solver (corner PDB 8!×3⁷ + two disjoint 6-edge PDBs 12P6×2⁶, admissible h = max of EXACT BFS distances rebuilt over the 27 STM generators) and, via his walking-distance heuristic, the 15-puzzle optimal solver (/scramble/solver 15p); the server-side cube48opt 3×3 optimal-solve daemon is in the same lineage
